task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Amateur' module has been deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Amateur' tab or window is no longer visible anywhere in the GPredict main interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new module named 'Thermal_Monitor' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tab or window explicitly named 'Thermal_Monitor' is visible in the GPredict interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the module tracks the ISS and CSS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The list view displays rows for exactly two satellites: 'ISS (ZARYA)' and 'CSS (TIANHE)'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Thermal_Monitor' module displays only a List View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Thermal_Monitor' module consists solely of a tabular list. There are no Map or Polar (radar) views visible within this module.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required columns are present in the List View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The column headers in the List View include 'Name', 'Vis', 'Orbit', 'Alt', and 'Footp'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the antenna pointing columns have been removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The column headers in the List View do NOT include 'Az', 'El', or 'Dir'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the application uses Imperial units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The numerical values in the 'Alt' (Altitude) and 'Footp' (Footprint) columns are followed by 'mi' (miles) instead of 'km' (kilometers).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GUI to configure the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show GPredict's graphical configuration dialogs (e.g., Preferences, Module settings) rather than terminal windows editing text files like gpredict.cfg or Amateur.mod.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual GPredict application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the standard GPredict application window, not a mock-up, text editor, or web browser.

integrity.description: Verify that the satellite data is genuinely populated by the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rows for ISS and CSS show realistic, calculated numerical data for Orbit, Alt, and Footp, rather than empty cells or placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Required module name: 'Thermal_Monitor'. Forbidden module: 'Amateur'. Required satellites: ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544) and CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274). The module must be configured to show ONLY a List View (no Map or Polar views). Required columns in the List View: Name, Vis, Orbit, Alt, Footp. Forbidden columns (must be removed): Az, El, Dir. Imperial units must be active globally, which is visually verifiable by checking if the Altitude (Alt) or Footprint (Footp) values end in 'mi' (miles) instead of 'km'.
